article_title: OpenAI, NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Intel and Microsoft Publish MRC 1.0 Through the OCP, an 800 Gb/s Multipath RDMA Protocol for AI Training
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the MRC 1.0 / OCP release (PR #1193, merged earlier in this batch). Third submission this batch on the same disclosure (#1193, #1195, #1201). The work is discarded under v3.9.0; not eligible for rewrite.

findings.details: Already-published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/08-openai-amd-broadcom-intel-microsoft-and-nvidia-release-mrc-10-to-ocp-an-open-rdma-protocol-already-running-openais-largest-training-clusters.md (originally PR #1193). Same MRC 1.0 / OCP release, same six-company collaborator list, same Fairwater + Abilene production deployments, same May 6 2026 announcement.

content_preview.title: OpenAI, NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Intel and Microsoft Publish MRC 1.0 Through the OCP, an 800 Gb/s Multipath RDMA Protocol for AI Training
content_preview.summary: Multipath Reliable Connection lets a single RDMA connection spread traffic across multiple network paths and reroute around failures in microseconds. It is already deployed at OCI Abilene and Microsoft Fairwater on NVIDIA Spectrum-X.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI, NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Intel and Microsoft have jointly released Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) as an open specification through the Open Compute Project, NVIDIA's [Gilad Shainer wrote](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/spectrum-x-ethernet-mrc/) on the NVIDIA blog. The protocol is already running in production on OpenAI's largest training clusters at the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure site in Abilene, Texas and at Microsoft's Fairwater supercomputers, [Network World reported](...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written; uses NVIDIA blog + OpenAI + Network World + AMD blog (latter two not used by PRs #1193/#1195). Adds the '800 Gb/s' speed in the title. On its own merits would APPROVE.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same MRC 1.0 release facts as the already-published article.
editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE of src/content/articles/2026-05/08-openai-amd-broadcom-intel-microsoft-and-nvidia-release-mrc-10-to-ocp-an-open-rdma-protocol-already-running-openais-largest-training-clusters.md (PR #1193). This is the third submission this batch on the same MRC disclosure (PRs #1193, #1195, #1201).
concerns: Direct duplicate of already-published article. Triple-collision in one batch (with #1193 and #1195) — same as the Anthropic/Colossus triple-collision. Bot operations needs stronger collision detection.
recommendations: Bot operations: parallel write-article agents need topic-collision detection that includes in-flight submissions, with a hard pre-check at the moment of branch creation.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1193 covers this disclosure.
